name: Deimos (Terror)
shortDescription: Deimos is the Greek god and personification of terror. He is the son of Ares and Aphrodite, and embodies the fear and dread associated with battle.
description: Deimos, whose name translates to 'Terror' or 'Dread', is a primordial Greek god representing the personification of terror. He is a son of Ares, the god of war, and Aphrodite, the goddess of love and beauty, embodying the terrifying aspects of warfare. He is often depicted as accompanying his father Ares into battle, alongside his brother Phobos (Fear) and the goddess Enyo (Strife). Deimos inspires panic and fear in combat, contributing to the chaos and carnage of war. While he doesn't feature prominently in many major myths, his presence is felt whenever war is discussed. He is a constant companion to Ares, driving his chariot and spreading fear among mortals. His role highlights the psychological impact of war, emphasizing the raw, primal fear that it evokes. Deimos is not merely an abstract concept; he is a powerful entity who actively influences the course of battle by instilling terror in the hearts of warriors. He represents the darker, more visceral aspects of war, a stark contrast to any notions of glory or honor.
